title: A2 · Input formats — student rows, counts, or aggregates?
text: Marco state-agency analyst. Three different districts handed three different files: District sent one row per student (student rows); District B sent pre-aggregated count rows (sufficient counts); District C sent publisher’s CSV (published aggregate). Marco needs estimate site-level rates three without spending day deciding sitemix function call. vignette decision tree. leave . end article able : Decide whether site-year data student rows, sufficient counts, published aggregate. Call matching entry function (sm_estimate(), sm_estimate_from_counts(), sm_estimate_from_aggregates()). Rely count-versus-rows equivalence guarantee source data already aggregated. Prerequisites. A1 · Getting started. example data. results computed live alprek_subset, anonymized, illustrative 50-site sample Alabama First Class Pre-K records shipped package (see ?alprek_subset), alprek_subset_counts sufficient-count path. real accountability dataset must cited empirical Pre-K results. Every code block runs offline fixed random seed.

title: A2 · Input formats — student rows, counts, or aggregates?
text: Use table pick entry point. Three questions disambiguate: “every row identify one student?” Yes → branch 1 (student rows). → branch 2 3. “every row carry explicit numerator denominator?” Yes → branch 2 (counts) 3 (aggregates). “data product publisher CSV known schema (e.g., state accountability files)?” Yes → branch 3. Otherwise → branch 2.

title: A3 · Scenario A — binomial estimates
text: end : Comfort three boundary methods ("wilson_floor", "agresti_coull", "none") pick . clear picture three reporting transforms ("arcsine", "logit", "none") — "arcsine" variance-stabilizing. role accountability_n flag_below_accountability flag supports reporting decisions. side--side count vs student-row demonstration confirms T2.5 identity numerically. title: A3 · Scenario A — binomial estimates
text: Boundary cells rows theta_raw exactly 0 1 (numerator equals 0 denominator). Boundary policy matters raw-scale output; default arcsine VST, row-level se remains closed-form arcsine SE se_raw records boundary-safe raw fallback. example uses vst = "none" var_method labels visible. Inspect sites look var_method column: raw-scale theta_raw 0 1, Wilson method records "wilson_boundary_surrogate" Agresti-Coull method records "agresti_coull_boundary_surrogate". Interior rows record "binomial" raw output. pick . Use "wilson_floor" (default, (Wilson, 1927)) accountability-style reports: floors SE small positive number without shifting theta_raw. Use "agresti_coull" (Agresti & Coull, 1998) standard z-general adjusted-Wald boundary uncertainty surrogate. methods retain observed theta_raw = C/n; neither replaces point estimate. Use "none" diagnostic comparisons (zero-cell SEs collapse).
code: wf <- sm_estimate(   subset(alprek_subset, year == 2021),   family          = "binomial",   indicator       = "tanf",   vst             = "none",   boundary_method = "wilson_floor" )  ac <- sm_estimate(   subset(alprek_subset, year == 2021),   family          = "binomial",   indicator       = "tanf",   vst             = "none",   boundary_method = "agresti_coull" ) side_by_side <- data.frame(   site_id      = wf$site_id,   n            = wf$n,   theta_raw    = wf$theta_raw,   wilson_se    = wf$se,   wilson_meth  = wf$var_method,   agresti_se   = ac$se,   agresti_meth = ac$var_method ) print(as.data.frame(head(side_by_side, 6)), row.names = FALSE) #>  site_id  n theta_raw  wilson_se               wilson_meth agresti_se #>     S001 10 0.0000000 0.07080048 wilson_boundary_surrogate 0.09292054 #>     S002  6 0.0000000 0.09957690 wilson_boundary_surrogate 0.12633581 #>     S003 14 0.0000000 0.05492723 wilson_boundary_surrogate 0.07337853 #>     S004 13 0.1538462 0.10006825                  binomial 0.10006825 #>     S005 17 0.0000000 0.04702080 wilson_boundary_surrogate 0.06335923 #>     S006 10 0.0000000 0.07080048 wilson_boundary_surrogate 0.09292054 #>                      agresti_meth #>  agresti_coull_boundary_surrogate #>  agresti_coull_boundary_surrogate #>  agresti_coull_boundary_surrogate #>                          binomial #>  agresti_coull_boundary_surrogate #>  agresti_coull_boundary_surrogate

title: A3 · Scenario A — binomial estimates
text: vst selects reporting scale theta_hat se via three choices: estimate_scale column records chosen scale. default "arcsine" variance-stabilizing choice; "logit" reporting transform useful downstream models expect log-odds; "none" retains raw proportion scale inspection direct reporting (see M2).
code: arc <- sm_estimate(subset(alprek_subset, year == 2024),                    family = "binomial", indicator = "frpm",                    vst = "arcsine") lgt <- sm_estimate(subset(alprek_subset, year == 2024),                    family = "binomial", indicator = "frpm",                    vst = "logit") raw <- sm_estimate(subset(alprek_subset, year == 2024),                    family = "binomial", indicator = "frpm",                    vst = "none") unique(arc$estimate_scale) #> [1] "arcsine" unique(lgt$estimate_scale) #> [1] "logit" unique(raw$estimate_scale) #> [1] "none"

what: 2. Scenario B vs Scenario C — the disambiguation
title: A4 · Scenarios B / C — multivariate and multinomial
text: Two questions order: “Can single student two indicators simultaneously?” Yes → Scenario B (overlapping binaries). → go question 2. “indicators partition population (student belongs exactly one)?” Yes → Scenario C (multinomial). Concrete examples: FRPM / SNAP / WIC / TANF: student can subset. Scenario B. Primary language ∈ {English, Spanish, }: student exactly one. Scenario C. two scenarios share structure “multiple components per site-year non-diagonal covariance,” differ covariance: Scenario B uses SUR-style cross-covariance joint proportions; Scenario C uses multinomial simplex covariance (diag(π) − π π') / n. scenarios accept student rows sm_estimate() complete sufficient counts sm_estimate_from_counts(). Scenario B count input must include every pairwise co-occurrence count; Scenario C count input must include every category count counts must sum n_jt. Published aggregate marginals separate D1 multivariate path never routed Scenario C.

title: A4 · Scenarios B / C — multivariate and multinomial
text: Call sm_estimate() family = "multivariate" indicators vector column names. Set vjt = TRUE attach per-row V list-column. site-year emits 4 rows (one per indicator). covariance across four indicators site S001 first element V list-column: vcov_scale caveat. Note vcov_scale = "raw": covariance matrix computed raw probability scale, even though theta_hat arcsine scale (default). SUR construction numerically stable raw space; matrix currently transformed arcsine-delta scale. practical implication: assume sqrt(diag(V)) == se. row-level se arcsine scale; matrix raw scale. Always read V[[]]$vcov_scale consuming matrix.
code: benefits_2024 <- sm_estimate(   subset(alprek_subset, year == 2024),   family     = "multivariate",   indicators = c("frpm", "snap", "wic", "tanf"),   vjt        = TRUE ) head(benefits_2024, 4) #> sitemix_estimates: 4 rows x 20 columns | family=multivariate | role=summary_uncertainty #> groups=1 sites=1 years=1 indicators=4 V=TRUE K=TRUE #> # A tibble: 4 × 20 #>   site_id  year indicator theta_raw theta_hat se_raw    se     n n_eff #>   <chr>   <int> <chr>         <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl> #> 1 S001     2024 frpm            0.4     0.685 0.155  0.158    10    10 #> 2 S001     2024 snap            0.6     0.886 0.155  0.158    10    10 #> 3 S001     2024 wic             0.9     1.25  0.0949 0.158    10    10 #> 4 S001     2024 tanf            0.1     0.322 0.0949 0.158    10    10 #> # ℹ 11 more variables: estimate_scale <chr>, transform <chr>, var_method <chr>, #> #   flag_small_n <lgl>, flag_zero_cell <lgl>, input_mode <chr>, #> #   flag_suppressed <lgl>, framing <chr>, flag_below_accountability <lgl>, #> #   V <list>, K <int> V_S001 <- benefits_2024$V[[1L]] round(as.matrix(V_S001), 4) #>       frpm  snap   wic  tanf #> frpm 0.024 0.016 0.004 0.006 #> snap 0.016 0.024 0.006 0.004 #> wic  0.004 0.006 0.009 0.001 #> tanf 0.006 0.004 0.001 0.009 V_S001$vcov_scale #> [1] "raw" V_S001$vcov_method #> [1] "sur"

title: A5 · Published aggregates D0 / D1
text: D1 case publisher reports several marginal indicators per site-year (e.g., FRPM rate + SNAP rate + WIC rate). marginal becomes row; cross-marginal correlation unidentified marginals alone, engine emits diagonal working-independence V (vjt = TRUE). Tell engine whether marginals describe observed units sampling_relation. Use "same_units" source documentation establishes common sample, "different_units" marginal samples known differ, default "unknown" otherwise. Equal denominators recorded denominator pattern; prove common units. Estimate: working-independence V matrix site S001: vcov_method = "working_independence" — -diagonals exactly zero cross-marginal covariance identified publisher marginals alone. formal raw pairwise intervals explicitly non-bound projected stress scenarios, see A7 · Variance smoothing Fréchet formal derivation M7 · Fréchet envelope theory.

what: 5. Subgroup files — Framing X vs Framing Y
title: A5 · Published aggregates D0 / D1
text: publisher file carries one row per (site, year, subgroup) triple, must pivot estimation. choice Framing X Framing Y depends question. helpers map fixed publisher total vocabulary , STUDENT(S), TOTAL, OVERALL canonical ; matching ignores case, surrounding whitespace, punctuation words. Framing X keeps publisher spelling source_subgroup audit using canonical composite key, Framing Y uses canonical indicator. Alias collisions fail duplicates. Mixed school/district/state rows routed automatically: split homogeneous reporting-level tables pivoting. Framing X — subgroup becomes site (composite site_id): Framing-X default keeps partition_target = "none" returns conditional rates. composition options "denominator_composition" "case_composition"; require complete category grid one canonical total every site-year. missing category row error, implicit zero. Suppressed numerators rule case composition, whereas denominator composition available every required denominator observed. Framing Y — subgroup becomes marginal indicator original site. Subgroups commonly describe different observational units, use sampling_relation = "different_units" unless source establishes otherwise: Pass either pivoted tibble sm_estimate_from_aggregates() appropriate family.
code: # Synthetic subgroup file (5 sites × 2 subgroups × 1 year) subgroups <- expand.grid(   site_id  = paste0("S", sprintf("%03d", 1:5)),   year     = 2024L,   subgroup = c("ell", "non_ell"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) subgroups$c_jt <- c(8, 4, 7, 5, 9, 3, 10, 6, 5, 8) subgroups$n_jt <- c(12, 6, 11, 9, 13, 7, 15, 10, 8, 12) head(subgroups, 4) #>   site_id year subgroup c_jt n_jt #> 1    S001 2024      ell    8   12 #> 2    S002 2024      ell    4    6 #> 3    S003 2024      ell    7   11 #> 4    S004 2024      ell    5    9 fx <- sm_pivot_subgroups_to_sites(   subgroups,   subgroup_col    = "subgroup",   numerator_col   = "c_jt",   denominator_col = "n_jt",   indicator       = "frpm_take_up" ) head(fx, 4) #> # A tibble: 4 × 9 #>   site_id     year indicator  c_jt  n_jt suppression_flag framing source_site_id #>   <chr>      <int> <chr>     <int> <int> <lgl>            <chr>   <chr>          #> 1 S001_ell    2024 frpm_tak…     8    12 FALSE            subgro… S001           #> 2 S001_non_…  2024 frpm_tak…     3     7 FALSE            subgro… S001           #> 3 S002_ell    2024 frpm_tak…     4     6 FALSE            subgro… S002           #> 4 S002_non_…  2024 frpm_tak…    10    15 FALSE            subgro… S002           #> # ℹ 1 more variable: source_subgroup <chr> fy <- sm_pivot_subgroups_to_indicators(   subgroups,   subgroup_col    = "subgroup",   numerator_col   = "c_jt",   denominator_col = "n_jt" ) head(fy, 4) #> # A tibble: 4 × 8 #>   site_id  year indicator source_subgroup  c_jt  n_jt suppression_flag framing   #>   <chr>   <int> <chr>     <chr>           <int> <int> <lgl>            <chr>     #> 1 S001     2024 ell       ell                 8    12 FALSE            subgroup… #> 2 S001     2024 non_ell   non_ell             3     7 FALSE            subgroup… #> 3 S002     2024 ell       ell                 4     6 FALSE            subgroup… #> 4 S002     2024 non_ell   non_ell            10    15 FALSE            subgroup…  est_fy <- sm_estimate_from_aggregates(   fy,   family = "multivariate",   indicators = attr(fy, "indicator_set"),   framing = "subgroup_as_indicator",   sampling_relation = "different_units" )

Key columns: n_cells — total rows. n_flag_small_n — rows min_n. n_flag_below_accountability — rows accountability_n. scalar_uncertainty_finite — retained estimates SEs finite. scalar_se_positive — retained scalar SEs strictly positive. exact SRSWOR census zero uncertainty design; sets fact FALSE classified note zero_uncertainty_census. indicator_scale_consistent — indicator uses one estimate scale. v_valid estimate_vcov_scale_compatible — validity scale compatibility V list-column present. suppression_sensitivity_role — either none explicit nonidentified_variance_sensitivity role. smoothing_v_relation v_stale — whether experimental scalar smoothing result absent, matching, incompatible, mixed relative V, whether matching-scale matrix left stale. severity priority used every level: sm_diagnose() calls ordinary sitemix_estimates validation first. summarizes validated object; substitute validation.

title: M2 · Scalar SE — binomial pipeline
text: Inside Scenario , row-level closed form Cjt∣njt,πjt∼Binomial(njt,πjt),π̂jt=Cjt/njt. C_{jt} \mid n_{jt}, \pi_{jt} \;\sim\; \mathrm{Binomial}(n_{jt}, \pi_{jt}), \qquad \hat\pi_{jt} = C_{jt}/n_{jt}. sm_estimate() returns π̂jt\hat\pi_{jt} theta_raw reported-scale estimate theta_hat. vignette derives SE column sjts_{jt} reporting transform, correction, finite-population branch, boundary policy.

title: M2 · Scalar SE — binomial pipeline
text: Proposition 1 (first-order arcsine VST). arcsine transform g(p)=arcsin⁡pg(p) = \arcsin\sqrt{p}, delta-method SE sjt=12njt. s_{jt} \;=\; \frac{1}{2\sqrt{n_{jt}}}. \tag{M2.1} Proof sketch. Compute g′(p)=1/(2p(1−p))g'(p) = 1/(2\sqrt{p(1-p)}) apply s=g′(π̂)⋅π̂(1−π̂)/ns = g'(\hat\pi) \cdot \sqrt{\hat\pi(1-\hat\pi)/n}. product simplifies; π̂(1−π̂)\hat\pi(1-\hat\pi) cancels. ▫\square Attribution. Classical variance-stabilizing transform; see Anscombe (1948) Anscombe correction. Proposition 2 (logit reporting transform). g(p)=log⁡(p/(1−p))g(p) = \log(p/(1-p)), delta-method SE sjt=1njtπ̂jt(1−π̂jt). s_{jt} \;=\; \sqrt{\frac{1}{n_{jt} \hat\pi_{jt} (1 - \hat\pi_{jt})}}. \tag{M2.2} Attribution. Standard delta-method application. logit variance-stabilizing: working SE still depends π̂jt\hat\pi_{jt}. Identity reporting scale. g(p)=pg(p) = p returns sjt=π̂jt(1−π̂jt)/njts_{jt} = \sqrt{\hat\pi_{jt}(1-\hat\pi_{jt})/n_{jt}}. identity also variance-stabilizing.

title: M2 · Scalar SE — binomial pipeline
text: Verify alprek_subset: seven invariants hold documented tolerances.
code: data(alprek_subset, package = "sitemix") est <- sm_estimate(   subset(alprek_subset, year == 2024),   family = "binomial", indicator = "frpm", vst = "arcsine" ) interior <- which(est$theta_raw > 0 & est$theta_raw < 1)  # T1: closed-form theta_hat manual_theta <- asin(sqrt(est$theta_raw[interior])) stopifnot(all.equal(est$theta_hat[interior], manual_theta, tolerance = 1e-10))  # T2: closed-form SE at interior rows manual_se <- 1 / (2 * sqrt(est$n[interior])) stopifnot(all.equal(est$se[interior], manual_se, tolerance = 1e-10))  # T2.5: sufficient-counts identity counts <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "alprek_subset_counts.rds",                               package = "sitemix")) est_counts <- sm_estimate_from_counts(   counts[counts$year == 2024,          c("site_id", "year", "n_jt", "c_jt_frpm")],   family = "binomial", indicator = "frpm" ) stopifnot(all.equal(est$theta_hat, est_counts$theta_hat, tolerance = 1e-10)) stopifnot(all.equal(est$se, est_counts$se, tolerance = 1e-10))  # T3/T4: use a tiny fixture so boundary and logit behavior are explicit toy <- data.frame(   site_id = c(rep("A", 10), rep("B", 10)),   year = 2024,   y = c(rep(1, 10), rep(c(1, 0), each = 5)) ) toy_raw <- sm_estimate(   toy, family = "binomial", indicator = "y",   vst = "none", boundary_method = "wilson_floor" ) z <- qnorm(0.975) manual_wilson <- z / (2 * 10 * (1 + z^2 / 10)) stopifnot(all.equal(toy_raw$se[toy_raw$site_id == "A"],                     manual_wilson, tolerance = 1e-10)) stopifnot(toy_raw$var_method[toy_raw$site_id == "A"] ==             "wilson_boundary_surrogate")  toy_logit <- sm_estimate(   subset(toy, site_id == "B"),   family = "binomial", indicator = "y", vst = "logit" ) stopifnot(all.equal(toy_logit$theta_hat, log(0.5 / 0.5), tolerance = 1e-10))  logit_boundary <- tryCatch(   sm_estimate(toy, family = "binomial", indicator = "y", vst = "logit"),   error = identity ) stopifnot(inherits(logit_boundary, "sitemix_error_estimate_var_method"))  # T5/T6: plug-in and design-corrected finite-population branches fpc_counts <- data.frame(   site_id = "F", year = 2024L, n_jt = 8L, c_jt_rate = 3L ) fpc_plugin <- sm_estimate_from_counts(   fpc_counts, family = "binomial", indicator = "rate",   vst = "arcsine", fpc = 20, min_n = 1L ) stopifnot(all.equal(   fpc_plugin$se,   (1 / (2 * sqrt(8))) * sqrt(12 / 19),   tolerance = 1e-12 )) stopifnot(all.equal(fpc_plugin$variance_multiplier_applied, 12 / 19,                     tolerance = 1e-12))  fpc_corrected <- sm_estimate_from_counts(   fpc_counts, family = "binomial", indicator = "rate",   vst = "arcsine", bias_correction = "binomial_bc",   fpc = 20, min_n = 1L ) stopifnot(all.equal(   fpc_corrected$se,   (1 / (2 * sqrt(7))) * sqrt(12 / 20),   tolerance = 1e-12 )) stopifnot(all.equal(fpc_corrected$variance_multiplier_applied, 12 / 20,                     tolerance = 1e-12)) stopifnot(identical(fpc_corrected$n_eff, 8))

title: M3 · Multivariate SUR covariance
text: Theorem 1 (population SUR cross-covariance). two overlapping binary indicators XkX_k Xk′X_{k'} measured nn IID students per site-year, Cov⁡(π̂k,π̂k′)=πkk′−πkπk′njt, \boxed{ \operatorname{Cov}(\hat\pi_k,\hat\pi_{k'}) \;=\; \frac{\pi_{kk'} - \pi_k\pi_{k'}}{n_{jt}}, } \tag{M3.1} πk=Pr⁡(Xk=1)\pi_k=\Pr(X_k=1), πk′=Pr⁡(Xk′=1)\pi_{k'}=\Pr(X_{k'}=1), πkk′=Pr⁡(Xk=1,Xk′=1)\pi_{kk'}=\Pr(X_k=1,X_{k'}=1) population probabilities. Proof sketch. Treat student Bernoulli trial 2K2^K-cell multinomial space. sample covariance two binary marginals reduces Cov(Xk,Xk′)/n\mathrm{Cov}(X_k, X_{k'})/n, simplifies . ▫\square Attribution. Standard SUR result; see Zellner (1962). package observe population probabilities (M3.1). substitutes empirical proportions. Q=∑(yi−y‾)(yi−y‾)⊤Q=\sum_i(y_i-\bar y)(y_i-\bar y)^\top, default -FPC matrix plug-estimate V̂plugin=Q/n2\widehat V_{\mathrm{plugin}}=Q/n^2; binomial_bc alternative Q/[n(n−1)]Q/[n(n-1)]. diagonal therefore recovers corresponding estimated Scenario raw-scale variance, unknown population variance . raw-scale matrix convention one used Scenario B sitemix (Lee, 2026). fixed population size NN sampled without replacement, define q={0,N=n,(N−n)/(N−1),N>n. q=\begin{cases} 0, & N=n,\\ (N-n)/(N-1), & N>n. \end{cases} first branch includes exact one-unit census N=n=1N=n=1. approved SRSWOR whole-matrix rules V̂plugin=qQ/n2,V̂design={0K×K,N=n,(N−n)Q/[Nn(n−1)],N>n n>1. \widehat V_{\mathrm{plugin}}=qQ/n^2,\qquad \widehat V_{\mathrm{design}}= \begin{cases} 0_{K\times K}, & N=n,\\ (N-n)Q/[Nn(n-1)], & N>n\text{ }n>1. \end{cases} \tag{M3.2} standard without-replacement sampling corrections (Cochran, 1977). second rule selected bias_correction = “binomial_bc”. scale entire matrix rather replacing diagonal, symmetry, PSD, correlation structure preserved. N = n valid census produces zero matrix. Wilson boundary surrogate remains named scalar/diagonal regularizer carries conventional qq multiplier. four implemented interior-cell cases therefore: multiplier applies every matrix entry. diagonal-correction change correlations implemented estimand. constant indicator, empirical cross-covariances exactly zero. boundary_method = "wilson_floor" may replace indicator’s zero diagonal positive, explicitly named scalar/diagonal surrogate, never creates -diagonal covariance. Agresti–Coull boundary regularization illegal vjt = TRUE.

code: data(alprek_subset, package = "sitemix") est <- sm_estimate(   subset(alprek_subset, year == 2024),   family = "multivariate",   indicators = c("frpm", "snap"),   vjt = TRUE ) V1 <- as.matrix(est$V[[1L]])  # SUR1: closed form cross-covariance slice <- subset(alprek_subset, year == 2024 & site_id == est$site_id[1L]) n <- nrow(slice) p_x <- mean(slice$frpm) p_y <- mean(slice$snap) p_xy <- mean(slice$frpm * slice$snap) manual_sigma <- (p_xy - p_x * p_y) / n stopifnot(all.equal(V1[1, 2], manual_sigma, tolerance = 1e-10))  # SUR2: PSD eig_V1 <- eigen(V1, symmetric = TRUE, only.values = TRUE)$values tol_V1 <- 64 * nrow(V1) * .Machine$double.eps * max(abs(eig_V1)) +   64 * nrow(V1) * .Machine$double.xmin stopifnot(min(eig_V1) >= -tol_V1)  # SUR3: lexicon stopifnot(est$V[[1L]]$vcov_method == "sur") stopifnot(est$V[[1L]]$vcov_scale == "raw") stopifnot(est$V[[1L]]$diag_contract == "row_se_raw_squared") stopifnot(est$estimate_scale[[1L]] == "arcsine") stopifnot(est$V[[1L]]$psd_repair == "none")

title: M4 · Multinomial simplex covariance
text: Proposition 1 (Multinomial covariance). multinomial sampling model KK mutually exclusive categories, Σjt=1njt(diag(πjt)−πjtπjt⊤). \boxed{ \Sigma_{jt} \;=\; \frac{1}{n_{jt}}\bigl(\mathrm{diag}(\pi_{jt}) - \pi_{jt}\pi_{jt}^{\top}\bigr). } \tag{M4.1} Proof sketch. Direct moment-generating function multinomial; variance Cjt,kC_{jt,k} nπk(1−πk)n \pi_k (1 - \pi_k) covariance Cjt,kC_{jt,k} Cjt,k′C_{jt,k'} (k≠k′k \ne k') −nπkπk′-n \pi_k \pi_{k'}. Divide n2n^2 obtain covariance matrix estimated category proportions. ▫\square Attribution. Classical multinomial-sampling theory; see Agresti (2013). fixed-population SRSWOR population size NN, write M=diag(π̂)−π̂π̂⊤M=\mathrm{diag}(\hat\pi)-\hat\pi\hat\pi^\top define q={0,N=n,(N−n)/(N−1),N>n. q=\begin{cases} 0, & N=n,\\ (N-n)/(N-1), & N>n. \end{cases} first branch includes N=n=1N=n=1. plug-design-corrected whole-matrix rules V̂plugin=qM/n,V̂design={0K×K,N=n,(N−n)M/[N(n−1)],N>n n>1. \widehat V_{\mathrm{plugin}}=qM/n,\qquad \widehat V_{\mathrm{design}}= \begin{cases} 0_{K\times K}, & N=n,\\ (N-n)M/[N(n-1)], & N>n\text{ }n>1. \end{cases} \tag{M4.2} finite-population factors follow standard SRSWOR theory (Cochran, 1977). design-corrected rule selected bias_correction = “binomial_bc”. Multiplying whole matrix preserves PSD simplex identity V𝟏=0V\mathbf 1=0; census (N = n) zero sampling covariance. covariance metadata records conventional FPC actually applied plug-/design multiplier without changing n_eff. Without supplied finite-population design, corresponding rules M/nM/n M/(n−1)M/(n-1).

code: counts <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "alprek_subset_counts.rds",                               package = "sitemix"))  # AG1: D0 = Scenario A d0 <- counts[counts$year == 2024, c("site_id", "year", "n_jt", "c_jt_frpm")] d0$indicator <- "frpm" d0$c_jt <- d0$c_jt_frpm d0 <- d0[c("site_id", "year", "indicator", "c_jt", "n_jt")]  est_a <- sm_estimate_from_counts(   counts[counts$year == 2024, c("site_id", "year", "n_jt", "c_jt_frpm")],   family = "binomial", indicator = "frpm" ) est_d0 <- sm_estimate_from_aggregates(   d0, family = "binomial", indicator = "frpm" ) canonical_numeric <- c("theta_raw", "theta_hat", "se_raw", "se", "n") stopifnot(all(vapply(canonical_numeric, function(field) {   isTRUE(all.equal(est_a[[field]], est_d0[[field]], tolerance = 1e-10)) }, logical(1)))) stopifnot(!identical(est_a$input_mode, est_d0$input_mode))  # AG2 + AG3: D1 working independence d1_long <- rbind(   data.frame(site_id = d0$site_id, year = d0$year,              indicator = "frpm",              c_jt = counts[counts$year == 2024, ]$c_jt_frpm,              n_jt = counts[counts$year == 2024, ]$n_jt),   data.frame(site_id = d0$site_id, year = d0$year,              indicator = "snap",              c_jt = counts[counts$year == 2024, ]$c_jt_snap,              n_jt = counts[counts$year == 2024, ]$n_jt) ) est_d1 <- capture_expected_sitemix_warning(   sm_estimate_from_aggregates(     d1_long, family = "multivariate",     indicator_col = "indicator",     sampling_relation = "same_units",     vjt = TRUE   ),   "sitemix_warning_working_independence_default" ) V1 <- as.matrix(est_d1$V[[1L]]) grp <- est_d1[est_d1$site_id == est_d1$site_id[1L] &                 est_d1$year == est_d1$year[1L], ] off_diag <- V1[upper.tri(V1)] stopifnot(max(abs(off_diag)) < 1e-12) stopifnot(est_d1$V[[1L]]$vcov_method == "working_independence") stopifnot(attr(est_d1, "sampling_relation") == "same_units") stopifnot(attr(est_d1, "denominator_pattern") == "common") stopifnot(attr(est_d1, "d1_regime") == "D1a") stopifnot(all.equal(unname(diag(V1)), unname(grp$se^2), tolerance = 1e-12))

code: data(alprek_subset, package = "sitemix") est <- sm_estimate(   subset(alprek_subset, year == 2024),   family = "binomial", indicator = "frpm" ) est_s <- sm_smooth_variance(   est, method = "loglinear", return_diagnostics = TRUE, min_rows = 2 )  # SM1: finite nonnegative smoothed SE stopifnot(all(is.finite(est_s$se_smoothed))) stopifnot(all(est_s$se_smoothed >= 0))  # SM2: default transformed-scale formula has no offset fit_formula <- formula(attr(est_s, "smoother_fit")) stopifnot(identical(deparse(fit_formula), "log_var ~ log_n"))  est_raw <- capture_expected_sitemix_warning(   sm_smooth_variance(     est, method = "loglinear", scale = "se_raw",     return_diagnostics = TRUE, min_rows = 2   ),   "sitemix_warning_raw_scale_smoothing" ) raw_formula <- formula(attr(est_raw, "smoother_fit")) stopifnot(identical(deparse(raw_formula),                     "log_var ~ log_n + offset(p_offset)"))  # SM3: default preserves se stopifnot(all.equal(est_s$se, est$se, tolerance = 1e-12))  # SM4: alternative provenance does not rewrite canonical provenance stopifnot(identical(est_s$var_method, est$var_method)) stopifnot("var_method_smoothed" %in% names(est_s))  # SM6: append-only smoothing preserves design-adjusted inputs and provenance counts <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "alprek_subset_counts.rds",                               package = "sitemix")) fpc_counts <- counts[counts$year == 2024,                      c("site_id", "year", "n_jt", "c_jt_frpm")] est_fpc <- sm_estimate_from_counts(   fpc_counts, family = "binomial", indicator = "frpm",   fpc = 2 * fpc_counts$n_jt, min_n = 1L ) est_fpc_s <- sm_smooth_variance(est_fpc, method = "loglinear", min_rows = 2L) fpc_fields <- c(   "population_size", "sampling_fraction",   "fpc_variance_multiplier", "fpc_se_multiplier",   "variance_multiplier_applied", "se_multiplier_applied",   "sampling_design", "variance_rule" ) stopifnot(identical(est_fpc_s$se, est_fpc$se)) stopifnot(identical(est_fpc_s$se_raw, est_fpc$se_raw)) stopifnot(all(vapply(fpc_fields, function(field) {   identical(est_fpc_s[[field]], est_fpc[[field]]) }, logical(1))))

text: Proposition 1 (Fréchet-Hoeffding bounds). binary marginals πx,πy∈[0,1]\pi_x, \pi_y \[0, 1], every joint proportion πxy\pi_{xy} consistent marginals satisfies max⁡(0,πx+πy−1)≤πxy≤min⁡(πx,πy). \boxed{ \max(0, \pi_x + \pi_y - 1) \;\le\; \pi_{xy} \;\le\; \min(\pi_x, \pi_y). } \tag{M7.1} Attribution. Classical Fréchet-Hoeffding bounds (Fréchet, 1951; Hoeffding, 1994); see also Nelsen (2006) §2.5. probability bounds imply pairwise correlation bounds. formal D1a, raw covariance endpoint σkk′=(πxy−πxπy)/njt\sigma_{kk'} = (\pi_{xy} - \pi_x \pi_y) / n_{jt} (M3.1). raw_pairwise_intervals stores two marginals, common denominator, joint probability endpoints, correlation endpoints, raw covariance endpoints directly. Formal D1a therefore requires explicit same_units provenance, one finite common denominator per site-year, IID plug-variance rule, FPC binomial_bc correction. heuristic D1b, justified common njtn_{jt}. object records n_common = NA constructs heuristic covariance range Vkk′corner=ρkk′cornersksk′, V_{kk'}^{\mathrm{corner}} = \rho_{kk'}^{\mathrm{corner}} s_k s_{k'}, sks_k sk′s_{k'} raw-scale row standard errors. covariance_construction interval_scope columns keep heuristic range separate formal /n result.

what: 4. D1a vs D1b — formal vs heuristic
title: M7 · Fréchet pairwise intervals and projected stress theory
text: D1a (formal). publisher marginals refer sampled units satisfy compatible denominator/design contract, raw pairwise interval formally valid: feasible pairwise joint distribution must lie interval. Equal denominators alone establish -unit provenance. D1b (heuristic). marginals subgroup- conditional (e.g., FRPM rate among ELL students vs FRPM rate among non-ELL students), marginals refer different populations Fréchet interval formal joint constraint. returned ranges projected matrices heuristic stress quantities explicit counterfactual dependence construction. sm_frechet_envelope() requires explicit acknowledgement distinction via population_regime argument ("d1a" "d1b").

what: 5. PSD projection creates scenarios, not bounds

code: counts <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "alprek_subset_counts.rds",                               package = "sitemix")) d1 <- counts[counts$year == 2024, ] d1_long <- rbind(   data.frame(site_id = d1$site_id, year = d1$year,              indicator = "frpm", c_jt = d1$c_jt_frpm, n_jt = d1$n_jt),   data.frame(site_id = d1$site_id, year = d1$year,              indicator = "snap", c_jt = d1$c_jt_snap, n_jt = d1$n_jt) ) est_d1 <- capture_expected_sitemix_warning(   sm_estimate_from_aggregates(     d1_long, family = "multivariate",     indicator_col = "indicator",     sampling_relation = "same_units",     vjt = TRUE   ),   "sitemix_warning_working_independence_default" ) env <- sm_frechet_envelope(est_d1, population_regime = "d1a")  V_indep <- as.matrix(env$V_independence[[1L]]) raw_intervals <- env$raw_pairwise_intervals V_negative <- as.matrix(env$projected_negative_dependence_stress[[1L]]) V_positive <- as.matrix(env$projected_positive_dependence_stress[[1L]])  # FR1: direct formal /n oracle row1 <- raw_intervals[raw_intervals$site_key == env$site_keys$site_key[[1L]], ][1L, ] stopifnot(all.equal(   row1$pairwise_covariance_lower,   (row1$joint_probability_lower - row1$p_1 * row1$p_2) / row1$n_common )) stopifnot(all.equal(   row1$pairwise_covariance_upper,   (row1$joint_probability_upper - row1$p_1 * row1$p_2) / row1$n_common ))  # FR2: PSD after repair (relative tolerance + machine-range floor) eig_negative <- eigen(V_negative, symmetric = TRUE, only.values = TRUE)$values eig_positive <- eigen(V_positive, symmetric = TRUE, only.values = TRUE)$values tol_negative <- env$psd_tol * max(abs(eig_negative)) +   nrow(V_negative) * .Machine$double.xmin tol_positive <- env$psd_tol * max(abs(eig_positive)) +   nrow(V_positive) * .Machine$double.xmin stopifnot(min(eig_negative) >= -tol_negative) stopifnot(min(eig_positive) >= -tol_positive)  # FR3: K=2 shortcuts preserve their source corners stopifnot(nrow(V_negative) == 2L) stopifnot(all.equal(   V_negative,   as.matrix(env$unprojected_negative_dependence_corner[[1L]]) )) stopifnot(all.equal(   V_positive,   as.matrix(env$unprojected_positive_dependence_corner[[1L]]) )) stopifnot(all.equal(diag(V_negative), diag(V_indep), tolerance = 1e-8)) stopifnot(all.equal(diag(V_positive), diag(V_indep), tolerance = 1e-8))

"identified" & is.finite(scalar$theta_hat) &   is.finite(scalar$se) & scalar$se > 0 stopifnot(diag_s$scalar_uncertainty_finite) stopifnot(diag_s$scalar_se_positive) stopifnot(diag_s$indicator_scale_consistent) stopifnot(sum(table(scalar$iv_eligible)) == nrow(est))  # UO7: finite-population design provenance is an all-or-none row block. fpc_fields <- c(   "population_size", "sampling_fraction",   "fpc_variance_multiplier", "fpc_se_multiplier",   "variance_multiplier_applied", "se_multiplier_applied",   "sampling_design", "variance_rule" ) fpc_counts <- data.frame(   site_id = "F", year = 2024L, n_jt = 8L, c_jt_rate = 3L ) est_fpc <- sm_estimate_from_counts(   fpc_counts, family = "binomial", indicator = "rate",   fpc = 20, min_n = 1L ) stopifnot(all(fpc_fields %in% names(est_fpc))) stopifnot(!anyNA(est_fpc[fpc_fields])) stopifnot(identical(est_fpc$n_eff, 8))  # UO3/UO5: diagnose first; align tuple keys and matrix coordinates rather than # incidental row order. diag_b <- sm_diagnose(est_b, verbose = FALSE) diag_b_row <- sm_diagnose(est_b, level = "row", verbose = FALSE) diag_b_vcov <- sm_diagnose(est_b, level = "vcov", verbose = FALSE) status_b <- if ("estimate_status" %in% names(diag_b_row)) {   diag_b_row$estimate_status } else {   rep("identified", nrow(diag_b_row)) } stopifnot(   diag_b$v_present,   diag_b$v_valid,   all(status_b == "identified"),   !any(diag_b_row$flag_suppressed),   !any(diag_b_row$suppression_sensitivity_role != "none"),   !identical(diag_b$smoothing_provenance_valid, FALSE), !any(diag_b_vcov$row_sum_zero_ok %in% FALSE),   !any(diag_b_vcov$diag_severity == "error") )  plain_b <- as.data.frame(est_b) first_key <- unique(plain_b[c("site_id", "year")])[1L, , drop = FALSE] take <- plain_b$site_id == first_key$site_id & plain_b$year == first_key$year block <- plain_b[take, , drop = FALSE] V_object <- block$V[[1L]] stopifnot(all(vapply(block$V, identical, logical(1), V_object))) stopifnot(identical(V_object$site_id, first_key$site_id)) stopifnot(identical(V_object$year, first_key$year)) stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(block$indicator)) stopifnot(setequal(block$indicator, V_object$indicator_order)) coordinate <- match(V_object$indicator_order, block$indicator) stopifnot(!anyNA(coordinate), length(coordinate) == nrow(block)) aligned <- block[coordinate, , drop = FALSE] V <- as.matrix(V_object) stopifnot(identical(aligned$indicator, V_object$indicator_order)) stopifnot(nrow(V) == ncol(V)) if ("K" %in% names(block)) {   stopifnot(all(block$K == nrow(V))) } else {   stopifnot(nrow(V) == 1L) } stopifnot(isTRUE(all.equal(V, t(V), tolerance = 1e-12))) stopifnot(identical(rownames(V), V_object$indicator_order)) stopifnot(identical(colnames(V), V_object$indicator_order))  # UO4/UO6: matrix validity and scale compatibility are separate facts. what: sitemix: Site- and group-level proportions, rates, and sampling uncertainty — sitemix-package
title: sitemix: Site- and group-level proportions, rates, and sampling uncertainty — sitemix-package
text: sitemix produces site- group-level point estimates, standard errors, optional covariance matrices student rows, sufficient counts, published aggregates. package covers five estimation scenarios ships diagnostics, publisher-side suppression auditing, optional variance smoothing, raw pairwise Fréchet intervals projected stress scenarios unidentified D1 covariance.

title: sitemix: Site- and group-level proportions, rates, and sampling uncertainty — sitemix-package
text: Five estimation scenarios dispatched sm_estimate(): Scenario — binomial One binary indicator per site-year. Input student rows via sm_estimate() sufficient counts via sm_estimate_from_counts(). Covariance matrix \(1 \times 1\). Scenario B — multivariate Overlapping binary indicators per site-year SUR-style covariance. Input student rows complete sufficient counts containing marginal pairwise co-occurrence counts. Covariance \(K \times K\). Scenario C — multinomial Mutually exclusive categories summing denominator. Simplex covariance analytic rank \(S - 1\), \(S\) positive observed support. Input student rows complete category counts, published D1 marginals. Scenario D0 — aggregate binomial Published numerator/denominator per site-year. Dispatch via sm_estimate(). Scenario D1 — aggregate marginal Multiple published marginals per site-year working-independence covariance. Dispatch via sm_estimate(). Use sm_frechet_envelope() sensitivity.

title: sitemix: Site- and group-level proportions, rates, and sampling uncertainty — sitemix-package
text: sm_estimate() Main dispatcher student rows, counts, aggregates. sm_estimate_from_counts() Sufficient-counts wrapper. sm_estimate_from_aggregates() Published-aggregates wrapper D0 / D1 dispatch. sm_diagnose() Uncertainty audit three levels (summary / row / vcov). sm_suppression_report() Publisher-side three-tier suppression audit.

title: Diagnose uncertainty in a sitemix_estimates tibble — sm_diagnose
text: One three S3-classed tibbles depending level; see Details. summary variant one object-level row adds one integer n_var_method_<label> column observed var_method; dynamic column set therefore depends diagnosed object. row vcov variants row-per-unit structures suitable filtering / dplyr operations.

title: Diagnose uncertainty in a sitemix_estimates tibble — sm_diagnose
text: three diagnostic levels return distinct S3 classes: level = "summary" Returns sitemix_diagnostics_summary tibble: one row summarizing object, including denominator percentiles, tier counts, intrinsic scalar/covariance validity facts. cheapest level; suitable interactive sanity check. level = "row" Returns sitemix_diagnostics_row tibble: one row per site-year-indicator every canonical flag described row-level severity tier. Use audit needs per-row provenance. level = "vcov" Returns sitemix_diagnostics_vcov tibble: one row per site-year-indicator block V list-column PSD margin (smallest eigenvalue), scale compatibility, smoothing relation, stale-matrix facts. meaningful x produced vjt = TRUE. Row diagnostics include canonical flags: flag_small_n flag_below_accountability. flag_zero_cell flag_suppressed. Summary diagnostics describe properties estimate object : finite scalar uncertainty, positive standard errors, indicator-level scale consistency, covariance validity, estimate/covariance scale compatibility. inspect assume downstream consumer. Diagnostic severity follows intrinsic four-level matrix. "error" records invalid scalar uncertainty, mixed scales within indicator, invalid smoothing provenance, stale matching-scale covariance. "warning" records unavailable suppression rows, non-identified variance sensitivity, explicit estimate/covariance scale mismatch. "note" records descriptive facts small denominators, boundary cells, accountability thresholds. "ok" means none facts applies. Severity priority error, warning, note, ok. diagnostic reports facts ordinary object validation; replace validate.sitemix_estimates(). summary field matrix grouped follows: Scalar facts scalar_uncertainty_finite, scalar_se_positive, indicator_scale_consistent. exact zero-uncertainty SRSWOR census reported separately note, error. Covariance facts v_present, v_valid, estimate_vcov_scale_compatible. Suppression facts Counts identified, suppressed-missing, sensitivity rows, plus role provenance, numeric-variance availability, acknowledgement. Smoothing facts smoothing_present, provenance validity, smoothing_v_relation, v_stale. Classification diag_severity semicolon-delimited diag_notes. suppression role-provenance field suppression_sensitivity_role.

Columns (order follows constructor R/output-assembly.R): site_id Character site identifier; first member keyed pair (site_id, year). year Integer year identifier; second member keyed pair. indicator Character scalar; indicator name passed via indicator = (Scenarios , D0) one indicator-component names (Scenarios B, C, D1). theta_raw Numeric \([0, 1]\); raw-scale proportion \(\hat\pi_{jt} = C_{jt}/n_{jt}\). theta_hat Numeric; point estimate scale named estimate_scale column. se_raw Numeric; raw-scale standard error VST. Finite non-negative estimable; NA intentionally suppressed/drop rows. se Numeric; scalar standard error estimate_scale. Finite non-negative estimable; NA intentionally suppressed/drop rows. n Positive integer; site-year denominator \(n_{jt}\). n_eff Numeric; transform-denominator provenance. Finite-population sampling change value. estimate_scale Character scalar; scale theta_hat se. One "none", "arcsine", "arcsine_anscombe", "logit". transform Character scalar; VST applied (typically equals estimate_scale). var_method Character scalar; row-level SE provenance, drawn implemented lexicon described Details. flag_small_n Logical; TRUE iff \(n_{jt} <\) min_n. flag_zero_cell Logical; TRUE identified boundary \(C_{jt} \\{0, n_{jt}\}\), FALSE identified interior, NA publisher suppression hides numerator. input_mode Character scalar; one "student_level", "counts_full_suff", "aggregate", recording dispatched input pathway. flag_suppressed Logical; TRUE iff site-year suppressed publisher (Tier 1 three-tier framework). title: Estimate site-year rates from published aggregate rows — sm_estimate_from_aggregates
text: D0 vs D1. aggregate-input dispatch follows Scenario taxonomy sm_estimate(): D0 Use family = "binomial" input one row per site-year explicit numerator_col denominator_col. D1 Use family = "multivariate" input multiple aggregate marginals per site-year. cross-indicator covariance identified marginals alone; engine emits diagonal working-independence V (vjt = TRUE). pairwise interval projected stress analysis unidentified joints, see sm_frechet_envelope(). Set aggregate_case = "auto" (default) resolve one unique indicator D0 two D1; pass "D0" "D1" assert case. D1 requires ordered indicator set every site-year group. Set sampling_relation = "same_units" marginal rows known describe observational units, "different_units" known differ. default "unknown" makes claim. Common denominators recorded separately never imply "same_units". Subgroup framings. publisher files site carries multiple subgroup rows, pivot file first via sm_pivot_subgroups_to_sites() (Framing X) sm_pivot_subgroups_to_indicators() (Framing Y), pass pivoted table . See vignette("a5-published-aggregates", package = "sitemix"). Suppression. wrapper exposes publisher-side controls: Detection: suppression_col, suppression_flag_value, suppression_when. Policy mode: suppression. Sensitivity point: suppressed_theta_hat. Required acknowledgement: suppression_sensitivity_acknowledge. Hidden denominators: suppressed_n_strategy suppressed_n_bound. audit pass estimation, use sm_suppression_report(). suppression = "drop" retains unavailable audit row canonical estimate SE columns missing. legacy "upper_bound" label now means separated worst-case variance sensitivity. suppression_sensitivity_acknowledge = TRUE required sensitivity; never populates canonical estimates ordinary covariance.

title: Compute D1 pairwise Fréchet intervals and projected stress scenarios — sm_frechet_envelope
text: Two interpretations: D1a D1b. function supports two population regimes caller must explicitly choose : D1a – formal common-population published marginals refer sampled units, use one finite denominator per site-year, use IID plug-raw-probability variance rule without FPC binomial_bc. conditions must already recorded D1 estimator. , raw pairwise covariance interval formal. matching denominator alone provenance. D1b – subgroup-conditional heuristic published marginals subgroup-conditional envelope heuristic stress test, formal bound. Requires explicit acknowledgement via subgroup_conditional_action = "allow" (proceeds warning "warn"); "error" aborts call. PSD stress scenarios. assembled corner matrices guaranteed PSD corners Fréchet box; function applies PSD projection via psd_method = "higham" (Higham 2002 nearest-PSD algorithm via Matrix::nearPD()) "shrink" (line search largest shrinkage weight yields PSD matrix). working-independence diagonal preserved repair. \(K \le 2\), projected fields exact identities source corners iterative method attempted. \(K > 2\), fixed shrink_alpha applied exactly even source corner already PSD; infeasible fixed value errors. Higham automatic-shrink nonconvergence also error rather returning partially repaired matrix. formal derivation see vignette("m7-frechet-envelope-theory", package = "sitemix"). Suppressed-missing suppression-sensitivity rows rejected entry: formal pairwise intervals require complete identified marginals.

title: Compute D1 pairwise Fréchet intervals and projected stress scenarios — sm_frechet_envelope
code: # D1 example using bundled counts as a synthetic aggregate slice counts_path <- system.file(   "extdata", "alprek_subset_counts.rds",   package = "sitemix", mustWork = TRUE ) counts <- readRDS(counts_path)  # Build a tiny D1 input: two marginals (frpm, snap), one year d1 <- counts[counts$year == 2024, ] d1_long <- rbind(   data.frame(     site_id   = d1$site_id, year = d1$year,     indicator = "frpm",     c_jt      = d1$c_jt_frpm, n_jt = d1$n_jt   ),   data.frame(     site_id   = d1$site_id, year = d1$year,     indicator = "snap",     c_jt      = d1$c_jt_snap, n_jt = d1$n_jt   ) )  est <- sm_estimate_from_aggregates(   d1_long, family = "multivariate",   indicator_col = "indicator",   sampling_relation = "same_units",   vjt = TRUE ) #> Warning: Working-independence default selected for D1 aggregate input. #> ✖ Expected `identified within-site covariance off-diagonals`. #> ℹ Actual: `off-diagonals structurally unidentified from marginal aggregates`. #> ℹ Fix: Use `sm_frechet_envelope()` once sensitivity diagnostics are available. env <- sm_frechet_envelope(est, population_regime = "d1a") class(env) #> [1] "sm_frechet_envelope" "list"                env$psd_method #> [1] "higham"

Two labels collapse "" site-year duplicates fail closed. Composition sums checked canonical total within partition_tolerance; missing category row never inferred structural zero. Mixed-level scope. Mixed school/district/state routing identified current public arguments. non-NULL level_override rtype_col therefore fails closed stable invalid-level condition documented level_override. Split mixed-level publisher files homogeneous tables calling either pivot helper.

title: Smooth standard errors with an experimental GVF model — sm_smooth_variance
text: Method choice. default method = "loglinear" lightweight stats::lm() fit. default scale = "se" path fits \(\log v_{jt} \sim \log n_{jt}\). scale = "se_raw", raw binomial variances also depend rate level, default adds boundary-safe offset \(\mathrm{offset}(\log(p^{\mathrm{}}_{jt}(1 - p^{\mathrm{}}_{jt})))\). alternative method = "gam" fits generalized additive model via mgcv::gam(); mgcv dependency runtime-guarded. Fay–Herriot area-level estimator: neither models point estimates produces small-area posterior means. Condition surface. Three warning classes emitted specific conditions: sitemix_warning_smoother_multi_year_default eligible rows span multiple years = NULL; helper pools years default warns. Use = "year" add year fixed effect. sitemix_warning_unexpected_slope fitted denominator slope deviates \(-1\) 0.15 loglinear arcsine smoothing. sitemix_warning_raw_scale_smoothing smoothing raw-scale SE; default raw-scale model includes rate-dependent p_offset term, custom formulas include analogous rate-dependent term. sitemix_error_invalid_smoothing_flag raised public logical smoothing control TRUE FALSE. Multi-year handling. default helper pools years single joint model. add year fixed effect (still one joint fit, per-year fits), pass = "year". obtain per-year smoothing, call function per year subset.
